text: Having coordinated multi-team releases for three years, I have come to view technical writing as a first-class engineering artifact rather than an afterthought. When we document trade-offs alongside metrics—latency budgets, error budgets, and rollout windows—newcomers reconstruct decisions without archaeological digs through chat history. I would argue that disciplined postmortems and bilingual glossaries further reduce coordination cost across remote cohorts, provided leadership protects time for reflection rather than treating it as optional overhead.
